chapters.caption: Mr Darcy dismisses Elizabeth Bennet at the Meryton assembly, declaring she is 'tolerable; but not handsome enough to tempt me.' Illustration by C.E. Brock for the 1895 Macmillan & Co. edition of Pride and Prejudice.
chapters.attribution: Charles Edmund Brock (1895), Pride and Prejudice illustrated edition, Macmillan & Co., London. Public domain.

chapters.caption: Elizabeth Bennet and Mr Darcy dancing together at the Netherfield ball, with Sir William Lucas also depicted. Illustration by Hugh Thomson for the 1894 George Allen edition of Pride and Prejudice.
chapters.attribution: Hugh Thomson (1894), Pride and Prejudice illustrated edition, George Allen, London. Public domain (Houghton Library, Harvard University).

chapters.caption: Mr Collins proposes to Elizabeth Bennet, declaring 'Almost as soon as I entered the house I singled you out as the companion of my future life.' Illustration by C.E. Brock for the 1895 Macmillan & Co. edition.
chapters.attribution: Charles Edmund Brock (1895), Pride and Prejudice illustrated edition, Macmillan & Co., London. Public domain.

chapters.caption: Mr Darcy makes his first proud proposal to Elizabeth Bennet at Hunsford, declaring 'You must allow me to tell you how ardently I admire and love you.' Illustration by C.E. Brock for the 1895 Macmillan & Co. edition.
chapters.attribution: Charles Edmund Brock (1895), Pride and Prejudice illustrated edition, Macmillan & Co., London. Public domain.

chapters.caption: Elizabeth Bennet contemplates the portrait of Mr Darcy at Pemberley, accompanied by Mrs Gardiner, Mr Gardiner, and housekeeper Mrs Reynolds. Illustration by C.E. Brock for the 1895 Macmillan & Co. edition (Chapter 43).
chapters.attribution: Charles Edmund Brock (1895), Pride and Prejudice illustrated edition, Macmillan & Co., London. Public domain.

chapters.caption: Lady Catherine de Bourgh confronts Elizabeth Bennet, demanding she renounce any engagement to Mr Darcy ('This is not to be borne, Miss Bennet'). Frontispiece engraving by Pickering & Greatbatch for the 1833 Richard Bentley edition — the first illustrated edition of Pride and Prejudice.
chapters.attribution: Pickering & Greatbatch (1833), Pride and Prejudice first illustrated edition, Richard Bentley, London. Public domain.
